violations.text: Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. The can opener was cleaned and sanitized during the time of the inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/14/2025

Baby formula was observed held past its disposition date. Food items shall be disposed of prior to their disposition date. Items were voluntarily disposed of during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/14/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the fan guards for the bakery room, dairy walk-in, and dairy walk-in floor have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment and facilities shall be cleaned at a frequency necessary to prevent the accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 10/14/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the fan guards in the 2-Door Reach-In have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Equipment is be cleaned as frequently as necessary to avoid the accumulation of soil residues New Violation. Correct By: 08/13/2024

Shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. New Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2024

Blocks of cheese and garlic spread were observed held at 45-48 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Blocks of cheese and the garlic spread were voluntarily disposed of at the of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2024
Logs of deli meat and cheese were missing the date with which they were opened for sale in deli case. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2024

Packages of imitation crab meat and lobster meat were observed held in walk-in cooler without any piercing or slicing of packaging to reintroduce oxygen into environment. Imitation crab meat was observed at 41 degrees Fahrenheit and was voluntarily disposed of. Lobster meat was still frozen and PIC sliced packaging at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2024

The cutting boards in the produce area and the meat department are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 09/07/2024
